965	// TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts verifies that execute-bead
966	// synthesizes a prompt with the bead's description, acceptance, and governing
967	// references, and emits the expected artifact bundle. Exercises real git +
968	// RealLandingGitOps via the script harness — no fakes for the components
969	// under test. The script harness is driven by a no-op directive; the prompt
970	// file synthesized by the orchestrator is read directly from the execution
971	// bundle on disk.
972	func TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts(t *testing.T) {
973		workDir := t.TempDir()
974	
975		scrubEnv := func() []string {
976			parent := os.Environ()
977			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
978			for _, kv := range parent {
979				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
980					continue
981				}
982				env = append(env, kv)
983			}
984			return env
985		}
986		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
987			t.Helper()
988			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
989			cmd.Dir = workDir
990			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
991			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
992			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
993			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
994		}
995	
996		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
997		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
998		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
999		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
1000		runGit("add", ".")
1001		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
1002	
1003		// Spec referenced via spec-id must exist in the committed tree so the
1004		// worker worktree (created by real `git worktree add`) sees it when the
1005		// orchestrator resolves governing refs.
1006		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feature.md")
1007		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
1008		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
1009	ddx:
1010	  id: FEAT-006
1011	---
1012	# Agent Service
1013	`), 0o644))
1014		runGit("add", "docs/feature.md")
1015		runGit("commit", "-m", "docs: add feature spec")
1016	
1017		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
1018			ID:          "my-bead",
1019			Title:       "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis",
1020			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
1021			Priority:    0,
1022			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
1023			Description: "Replace the bare fallback prompt with deterministic bead context.",
1024			Acceptance:  "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.",
1025			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "phase:build"},
1026			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-006"},
1027		})
1028		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
1029		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
1030		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
1031	
1032		// Script harness directive: no-op. The assertion target is the synthesized
1033		// prompt file, not any worker output. A no-op directive keeps the run
1034		// deterministic while still exercising the real runner + real git path.
1035		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
1036		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("no-op\n"), 0o644))
1037	
1038		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
1039		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1040		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
1041	
1042		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1043		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
1044			"--from", baseSHA,
1045			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
1046		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
1047		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
1048	
1049		require.NotEmpty(t, res.PromptFile)
1050		promptPath := filepath.Join(workDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.PromptFile))
1051		require.FileExists(t, promptPath)
1052		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(promptPath)
1053		require.NoError(t, err)
1054		promptText := string(promptRaw)
1055		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis")
1056		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Replace the bare fallback prompt")
1057		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.")
1058		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "docs/feature.md")
1059		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "Work on bead my-bead.")
1060	
1061		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir)
1062		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ManifestFile)
1063		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultFile)
1064		assert.True(t, strings.HasSuffix(res.PromptFile, "prompt.md"))
1065		assert.True(t, strings.HasSuffix(res.ManifestFile, "manifest.json"))
1066		assert.True(t, strings.HasSuffix(res.ResultFile, "result.json"))
1067	}

1145	func TestExecuteBeadFromRevFlag(t *testing.T) {

1158	// TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery verifies that worktrees matching the bead's
1159	// prefix are cleaned up at the start of a new run.
1160	func TestExecuteBeadOrphanRecovery(t *testing.T) {
1161		workDir := t.TempDir()
1162		orphanPath := workDir + "/.ddx/" + agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + "my-bead-old-attempt"
1163		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1164			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1165			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111",
1166			worktrees:   []string{orphanPath},
1167		}
1168		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1169		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
1170		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
1171		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
1172		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
1173	
1174		root := f.NewRootCommand()
1175		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json")
1176		require.NoError(t, err, "output: %s", out)
1177	
1178		// The orphan worktree should have been removed.
1179		assert.Contains(t, git.removedWTs, orphanPath,
1180			"orphan worktree should be removed before the new run")
1181	}

1183	// TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized verifies that when the agent makes no
1184	// real commits but the worktree is dirty with only harness bookkeeping files
1185	// (e.g. .ddx/agent-logs), SynthesizeCommit returns (false, nil) and the outcome
1186	// is "no-changes", not "merged" or "success". ResultRev must equal BaseRev.
1187	func TestExecuteBeadHarnessNoiseNotSynthesized(t *testing.T) {
1188		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1189			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1190			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // agent made no real commits
1191			wtDirty:     true,       // worktree is dirty (e.g. agent-logs written)
1192			// synthRev is intentionally empty: SynthesizeCommit returns (false, nil)
1193			// simulating that all dirty files were harness noise.
1194		}
1195		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
1196		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1197	
1198		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1199	
1200		assert.Equal(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome, "harness-noise-only dirty worktree must not produce a synthesis commit")
1201		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
1202		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
1203		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.ResultRev, "ResultRev must equal BaseRev when no real commit was made")
1204		assert.Equal(t, 0, git.mergeCalls, "merge must not be called when outcome is no-changes")
1205	}
1206	
1207	// TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits verifies that when the agent runner returns
1208	// an error and makes no commits, the outcome is an execution error rather than
1209	// a misleading no-change result.
1210	func TestExecuteBeadAgentErrorNoCommits(t *testing.T) {
1211		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
1212			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
1213			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // no commits made
1214		}
1215		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{err: fmt.Errorf("agent crashed"), result: nil}
1216		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
1217	
1218		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
1219	
1220		assert.Equal(t, 1, res.ExitCode)
1221		assert.Equal(t, "error", res.Outcome)
1222		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, res.Status)
1223		assert.Equal(t, "agent crashed", res.Reason)
1224		assert.Equal(t, "agent crashed", res.Error)
1225		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
1226		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
1227	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"

16	)
17	
18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}
59	
60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	// Repo setup
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}

110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	// Directive file helper
112	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	// writeDirectiveFile writes a directive file at path with the given lines.
115	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {
116		t.Helper()
117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755))
118		content := strings.Join(lines, "\n") + "\n"
119		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644))
120	}
121	
122	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
230	// Git assertion helpers
231	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	
233	// gitCommitCount returns the number of commits reachable from ref (plus any
234	// additional git rev-list args like "--not", "SHA").
235	func gitCommitCount(t *testing.T, projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) int {
236		t.Helper()
237		args := append([]string{"rev-list", "--count"}, refAndArgs...)
238		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, args...)
239		n, err := strconv.Atoi(out)
240		require.NoError(t, err, "git rev-list --count %v", refAndArgs)
241		return n
242	}
243	
244	// gitHasMergeCommits returns true when any merge commits exist in ref's history.
245	func gitHasMergeCommits(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
246		t.Helper()
247		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--merges", "--format=%H", ref)
248		return out != ""
249	}
250	
251	// refExists returns true when the given git ref is present in the repo.
252	func refExists(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
253		t.Helper()
254		_, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref)
255		return err == nil
256	}
257	
258	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	// Store helpers
260	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	// makeLoopStore creates an ExecuteBeadLoopStore backed by a bead.Store rooted
263	// in ddxDir. The store is already initialised by newScriptHarnessRepo.
264	func makeLoopStore(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) ExecuteBeadLoopStore {
265		t.Helper()
266		return bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
267	}
268	
269	// countClosedBeads counts how many beads in ddxDir have status "closed".
270	func countClosedBeads(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) int {
271		t.Helper()
272		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
273		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
274		require.NoError(t, err)
275		n := 0
276		for _, b := range all {
277			if b.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
278				n++
279			}
280		}
281		return n
282	}
283	
284	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	// Shell quoting helper
286	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	// escapeForShell escapes a string for embedding in a single-quoted shell argument.
289	func escapeForShell(s string) string {
290		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, "'", `'\''`)
291	}
292

24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
30		headRevSeq []string
31		headRevIdx int
32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
35		wtDirty bool
36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int
51		mergeRev   string
52	}
53	
54	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
55		f.mu.Lock()

57		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {

59				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
60			}

input.old_string:
func TestExecuteBeadResolvesPathStyleSpecID(t *testing.T) {
	workDir := t.TempDir()
	specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "workflows", "README.md")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte("# Workflow\n"), 0o644))
	refs := agent.ResolveGoverningRefs(workDir, &bead.Bead{
		ID:    "path-bead",
		Title: "Resolve path style spec ids",
		Extra: map[string]any{"spec-id": "workflows/README.md"},
	})
	require.Len(t, refs, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "workflows/README.md", refs[0].ID)
	assert.Equal(t, "workflows/README.md", refs[0].Path)
}

test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts to real git + script harness [ddx-ec830159]

Replaces fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with a real git repo, the
real agent.Runner, and the script harness driven by a directive file.
The synthesized prompt is read from the on-disk execution bundle rather
than captured via the fake runner's opts.

Per concerns.md §testing: no mocks for the thing under test. Pre-existing
installation-acceptance test failures (requiring a pre-built cli/build/ddx
binary not present in the execute-bead worktree) are excluded; the
targeted test passes: go test ./cmd -run '^TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts$' -count=1.
